definition: Jenkins is an open-source automation server used for continuous integration (CI) and continuous delivery (CD). It helps automate various stages of the software development lifecycle, including building, testing, and deploying applications.
service_description: The Jenkins Managed Service, can be quoted or ordered using the following product SKU's :
service_summary: This document describes the services the Open Systems team provides for the Jenkins Managed Service offering.
standard services:
Generic description The Open Systems team is committed to ensuring the availability of Jenkins services within the shortest possible timeframe, adhering to SLA, RTO, and RPO targets, while considering the inherent limitations of the team. All activities described below are part of the management of the service and directly linked to the Jenkins - Namespace Management (recurring) - SKU, which is billed on a recurring monthly basis. We will install and configure the Jenkins infrastructure to ensure it is ready for end-user use. However, any configuration within the application itself remains the responsibility of the end user. Our primary objective is to provide technical application support, which includes regular updates and patch management. Major version upgrades, however, are excluded from the scope of standard support and can be carried out under a separate, billable Request for Change (RFC). For scheduled activities, predefined service windows will be utilized. During these periods, Jenkins may experience temporary unavailability. All planned interruptions will be communicated in advance through the built-in banner feature within Jenkins. Note: theese SLA's are not applicable for bugs in community supported tools (Jenkins). | Environment | Day | Hours | | Test | Every 1st Tuesday of the month | 07:00 - 09:00 | | Production | Every 3rd Tuesday of the month | 07:00 - 09:00 |
Environment Day Hours Test Every 1st Tuesday of the month 07:00 - 09:00 Production Every 3rd Tuesday of the month 07:00 - 09:00 Backup and recovery Each Jenkins instance is backed up using an Image-Level Backup solution in accordance with the “Cegeka - O.Open Systems - Preferred 1 month replicated” backup policy. Monitoring and event resolution Each Jenkins instance is monitored using standard Cegeka monitoring tools, which include: Instances covered under this service are subject to both reactive and proactive monitoring. This is based on various criteria and triggering events, which may result in incidents being assigned to the Open Systems team. Monitoring-related incidents are managed according to their priority and within the designated service windows. Security baseline compliancy As with any server provided by our team, each Jenkins instance will have the latest approved Cegeka security baseline applied during the initial setup. Updates to the security baseline will be implemented via a Request for Change (RFC) when necessary. Capacity Management Cegeka ensures operational capacity monitoring for storage and compute resources through monitoring events. When predefined standard thresholds are exceeded, monitoring incidents are triggered with the appropriate priority for further action. Lifecycle Management Both the operating system and the Jenkins service will be patched regularly in alignment with Open Systems standards. Patching will follow the established cycles used for all managed servers and adhere to the DTAP (Development, Testing, Acceptance, Production) process. For Jenkins, the latest stable version will always be maintained. Minor and major updates will be applied systematically, ensuring that outdated versions of Jenkins are never in use. User Management User management is conducted in compliance with the prevailing security baselines, which include the following measures: Every new users access needs to be approved by an administrator. Access and permissions will be granted based on the engineers resolver group. Jenkins environment Within the Jenkins instance, each Competence Center (or resolver group, if no Competence Center is defined) is provided with an isolated environment. A recommended setup is outlined in our Best Practice - Jenkins guidelines1. While adherence to these recommendations is not mandatory, deviations may limit the availability of optional services. Outside the designated Competence Center group, creating additional groups is not permitted, ensuring enforcement of the predefined setup. 1 the corresponding document is stored in the Confluence space of the Open Systems team and if needed can be requested through the DA. Liability As Jenkins is an open-source tool, we cannot guarantee any SLA, RTO, or RPO commitments if the root cause of faulty behavior is attributable to a bug within the Jenkins software.
optional services:
Service accounts
Service accounts can be created for granting access to the Jenkins API.
Although this type of request is rather exceptional are rarely applies, the creation of additional service accounts can/should be requested/raised by creating a support ticket, as there is no automation supporting it.
